cover:
A high-quality architectural system block diagram illustrating the "CASE TRIAGE AGENT — STEP BY STEP".
Overall Theme: Technical architecture map, clean flow chart, analytical, educational, modern and well-structured layout.
Background: A very clean, subtle light gray background with faint grid lines.

Header:
- At the top-center, a massive dark bold rounded header block displaying "CASE TRIAGE AGENT — STEP BY STEP".
- Below the title, a small subtitle: "Your First Production AI Agent: claude-sonnet-4-7 + Salesforce API v64.0".
- In the top-right corner, a small circular profile icon labeled "Bennie Joseph | Architect".

Architecture Layers (Arranged hierarchically with clear connections):
- A "Step 1: Read Case Context" container (colored in neon blue) containing modules: "Case Subject + Description", "Account + Entitlement", "Open Case History".
- A "Step 2: Agent Reasons (claude-sonnet-4-7)" container (colored in neon green) containing modules: "Classify Issue Type", "Check Entitlement", "Draft Response", "Propose Update".
- A "Step 3: Validate + Commit" container (colored in grey) containing modules: "Confidence Gate", "Apex Idempotency", "Audit Log", "Human Review if < 0.85".
- A central block at the heart of the system labeled "runCaseTriageAgent()" with a rotating arrow icon. This central node has multiple dotted directional connector lines feeding into and out of all surrounding layers.
- An "INTEGRATION LAYER" box on the upper right with modules "Anthropic API anthropic-version: 2026-01-01" and "Salesforce REST v64.0".
- A bottom panel labeled "FIRST AGENT CHECKLIST" with sub-modules "One tool at a time", "Log every step", "Gate every write".

Style Details: Technical blueprint style, clean rounded rectangle containers, precise dotted lines with arrowheads showing control and data flow, soft pastel color scheme (pale yellows, soft blues, light greens, and dark blues), micro vector icons inside each block, neat modern sans-serif fonts, absolutely crisp rendering, no blurred elements, zero spelling mistakes, publication-ready engineering diagram.
images:
A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "APEX AGENT CLASS DIAGRAM".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "APEX AGENT CLASS DIAGRAM".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "Clean TypeScript + Apex Architecture for Your First Salesforce AI Agent" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Separation of Concerns" with a layers icon, "Testable in Isolation" with a shield icon, "Swappable LLM" with a swap icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "STARTER MISTAKE — All Logic in One Class". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "PRODUCTION PATTERN — Layered Architecture". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    class CaseAgent {
  async run(caseId: string) {
    // reads, reasons, writes all in one method
    // 400 lines, impossible to test, no confidence
    await this.doEverything(caseId)
  }
}
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    class CaseTriageAgent {
  constructor(
    private reader: CaseContextReader,
    private llm: ClaudeClient,       // sonnet-4-7
    private writer: ApexCaseWriter,  // Apex v64.0
  ) {}
  async run(caseId: string): AgentResult
}

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Reader owns Salesforce data access — no LLM calls", "Agent class owns reasoning loop — no DML", "Writer owns Apex commit — no LLM knowledge", "Test each layer independently with mocks", "Add confidence gate before writer.commit()".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "claude-sonnet-4-7", "Clean Architecture", "Testability", "Apex v64.0" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.

A highly detailed, technical blueprint-style developer infographic poster on the theme of "AGENT QUALITY METRICS".
Overall Theme: High-tech dark mode developer dashboard, cybersecurity blueprint, with sharp glowing lines and neat borders.
Colors: Deep dark navy background with glowing neon blue, electric cyan, warning red, and success green highlights.

Header:
- A prominent top-center glowing blue header: "AGENT QUALITY METRICS".
- Below it, a clean subtitle: "How to Know If Your First Salesforce Agent Is Actually Working" with a small Salesforce logo on the top-left and an "APEX BEST PRACTICES" badge on the top-right.

Section 1: "WHY IT MATTERS" (Top Row, 3 Horizontal Cards)
- "Escalation Rate" with a chart icon, "Latency P95" with a clock icon, "Commit Success" with a checkmark icon

Section 2: "THE COST" (Middle Row, Side-by-Side Comparison)
- Left Box (Red Theme): "WRONG: Measure Model Accuracy Only". Shows a red status pill "DANGER — ANTI-PATTERN".
- Right Box (Green Theme): "RIGHT: Measure Workflow Quality". Shows a green status pill "SUCCESS — BEST PRACTICE".

Section 3: "CODE PATTERNS" (Main Center)
- Left Column (Bad Code in Red Border):
  - Heading: "// BAD"
  - Code block:
    // Only checking: did the LLM say the right thing?
const accuracy = correctAnswers / totalCases
// Misses: latency, cost, escalation rate,
// hallucinations on Salesforce field names,
// failed Apex commits, duplicate writes
- Right Column (Good Code in Green Border):
  - Heading: "// GOOD"
  - Code block:
    const metrics = {
  humanEscalationRate: 0.12,   // target < 15%
  avgAgentLatencyMs: 3200,     // target < 5000
  confidenceP50: 0.91,         // target > 0.85
  apexCommitSuccessRate: 0.99, // target > 0.99
  costPerRun: 0.003,           // track weekly
}

Section 4: "DEVELOPER PROFILE & CHECKLIST" (Bottom Row)
- Left Side: A 3D Pixar-style cartoon avatar portrait of a dark-skinned South Asian male with a full thick black beard, shoulder-length wavy dark hair pulled up into a messy top bun, warm friendly smile showing teeth, wearing a grey crew-neck sweatshirt with the word "MAGNETIC" on it, set against a warm orange bokeh background. The avatar is labeled "Bennie Joseph | Salesforce Architect". Next to it is a quote bubble: "Architecting intuitive systems, engineering scalable solutions."
- Right Side: A checklist with points: "Track human escalation rate — target < 15%", "Track agent latency P95 — target < 5 seconds", "Track confidence score distribution weekly", "Track Apex commit failures and retry rates", "Track cost per agent run — alert on regressions".
- Footer: A thin bar with icons for "Observability", "Cost Tracking", "Quality Gates", "Weekly Review" and the text "CODE SMART. AUTOMATE FASTER. DELIVER EXCELLENCE."

Style Details: Super high resolution, technical drawing grid, razor-sharp white and neon text, crisp layout, developer IDE aesthetic, flat vector graphics with subtle glow, highly legible monospace font for code blocks, professional presentation.
